events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 19 March: Civilians bear the brunt as strikes and displacement rise
events.description: Ongoing strikes across the Middle East stemming from the US-Israeli bombing of Iran and subsequent counterstrikes in the Gulf by Tehran continue to drive humanitarian needs, civilian casualties and mass displacement. Concerns are also growing over maritime security, the economic crisis centred around the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, attacks on healthcare and the protection of critical infrastructure, including nuclear facilities. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 19 March: Civilians bear the brunt as strikes and displacement rise

events.title: Jihadist violence in Nigeria and DRC rose sharply last year even as global deaths from terror fell
events.description: Nigeria had largest increase in terrorism-related deaths, ranking fourth in global index behind Pakistan, Burkina Faso and Niger Jihadist violence rose sharply in Nigeria and Democratic Republic of Congo last year, even as global deaths from terrorism dropped to their lowest level in a decade, according to a new report. Nigeria recorded the largest increase in terrorism deaths globally in 2025, with fatalities rising by 46% from 513 in 2024 to 750, placing it fourth in the Global Terrorism Index, behind Pakistan, Burkina Faso and Niger. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Jihadist violence in Nigeria and DRC rose sharply last year even as global deaths from terror fell

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 23 March: Civilians bear brunt of ongoing war
events.description: The war in the Middle East is well into its fourth week and the humanitarian emergency it has sparked continues to be the focus of international attention, along with the energy crisis caused by attacks on shipping in key Strait of Hormuz and other oil and gas infrastructure in the Gulf region. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 23 March: Civilians bear brunt of ongoing war

events.title: China has been preparing for a global energy crisis for years. It is paying off now
events.description: As other Asian economies race to conserve energy, China has huge reserves of oil and gas as well as alternative energy sources like wind and solar Middle East crisis – live updates Xi Jinping has been preparing for a crisis like this for years. China must secure its energy supply “in its own hands”, its president was reported to have said during a visit to one of its vast oilfields in 2021. The US-Israel war on Iran plunged the Middle East into a deep conflict, with the strait of Hormuz – one of the most important waterways in global trade – all but closed and key energy facilities across the region under attack. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 China has been preparing for a global energy crisis for years. It is paying off now

events.title: War in the Middle East: Iran nuclear facility hit as equivalent of ‘one classroom of children’ killed, wounded daily in Lebanon
events.description: More than 1,000 people have been killed and 2,584 injured in Lebanon since the start of the US-Israel war on Iran, UN officials said Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War in the Middle East: Iran nuclear facility hit as equivalent of ‘one classroom of children’ killed, wounded daily in Lebanon

events.title: Strike on Sudan hospital kills at least 64 and wounds 89 more, WHO reports
events.description: Victims of army drone attack on East Darfur health facility included children and medical personnel A strike on a healthcare facility in Sudan has killed 64 people and wounded 89 more, the World Health Organization reported on Saturday. The UN’s humanitarian office in Sudan had earlier said it was “appalled by the attack on a hospital in East Darfur yesterday, reportedly killing dozens, including children, and injuring more”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Strike on Sudan hospital kills at least 64 and wounds 89 more, WHO reports

events.title: SA premier warns One Nation poses threat to federal Labor as Marles says party only ‘about stunts and the vibe’
events.description: Federal Coalition tells Pauline Hanson’s party to expect more policy scrutiny after historic result in South Australia election Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Albanese government has sharpened its attacks on One Nation as a party of “stunts and the vibe” after the South Australian premier warned Pauline Hanson is a threat to Labor following its historic state election result. The federal Coalition is also dialling up the pressure, warning One Nation to expect more scrutiny of its policy positions as it attempts to avert a SA-style collapse in other parts of the country. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SA premier warns One Nation poses threat to federal Labor as Marles says party only ‘about stunts and the vibe’

events.title: UK police investigates arson attack against Jewish charity ambulances
events.description: The UK police are looking for three suspects believed responsible for an overnight attack on ambulances belonging to a Jewish volunteer charity in London. No injuries were reported, but it is currently being treated as an antisemitic crime. The investigation is being led by the counter-terror officers, although it is not treated as a terrorist attack at this stage. Authorities say they are also looking into claims of responsibility made by a group online.
events.ticker_label: 💥 UK police investigates arson attack against Jewish charity ambulances

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Ukraine in spotlight as Russian strikes surge
events.description: The Security Council is meeting for an open briefing on Ukraine, requested by European members following Kyiv’s 18 March letter citing a surge in Russian strikes. Inside the country, attacks continue to take a heavy toll: since 19 March, at least 25 civilians have reportedly been killed and more than 130 injured, including children, particularly in Donetsk, Sumy and Zaporizhzhia. We’ll have live coverage below, app users can follow here – and for all our key meetings coverage each day, go here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Ukraine in spotlight as Russian strikes surge

events.title: Planet is storing more heat than it can release, UN warns
events.description: The amount of heat trapped by the Earth has reached record levels. According to the UN's annual State of the Climate report, published this Monday, the last 11 years have been the warmest ever recorded. For the first time, the World Meteorological Organization is warning of a growing "energy imbalance", driven by rising greenhouse gas emissions. Scientists also fear that a natural warming phase called El Niño could soon bring further heat records. FRANCE 24's Environment Editor Valerie Dekimpe explains.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Planet is storing more heat than it can release, UN warns

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 20 March: Energy shocks deepen as strikes hit infrastructure
events.description: The crisis in the Middle East continues, with strikes and counter-strikes reported across the region, driving civilian casualties, displacement and rising humanitarian needs. Energy assets in Gulf States continue to be hit, with serious long-term damaged reportedly sustained at Qatar's Ras Laffan gas hub. Oil prices remain above $100 a barrel, underscoring the growing global economic impact. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 20 March: Energy shocks deepen as strikes hit infrastructure

events.title: WHO verifies deadly hospital attack in war-torn Sudan
events.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) verified on Saturday a hospital attack in war-torn Sudan that killed 64 people.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO verifies deadly hospital attack in war-torn Sudan

events.title: Tehran has responded to attacks with speed and strategy - Iranians are bracing for a long war
events.description: Living under the threat of bombardment is terrifying. Barely an hour passes in Tehran without the sound of an explosion. Nowhere feels safe.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Tehran has responded to attacks with speed and strategy - Iranians are bracing for a long war

events.title: Jewish security organization working to keep its community safe amid elevated terror threat
events.description: Jewish security organizations, like Magen Am, are working to train volunteers on how to respond to an attack as the potential threat rises amid the conflict in the Middle East.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Jewish security organization working to keep its community safe amid elevated terror threat

events.title: How the Iran war is about to hit your wallet
events.description: Strikes on gas sites in the Iran war are driving up energy costs, pushing up prices for power, food and more worldwide.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How the Iran war is about to hit your wallet

events.ticker_label: 💥 Analysts say Gaza 'civilian' deaths include Hamas, other terror members working as medics, media workers

events.title: At least 14 people killed in fire at South Korean car parts factory
events.description: Almost 60 injured in blaze in Daejeon with footage seemingly showing people jumping from burning building to escape A fire at a car parts factory in South Korea has killed 14 people and injured almost 60 others. Firefighters said all of the missing are now accounted for after a search operation of the wreckage of the three-storey building. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚑 At least 14 people killed in fire at South Korean car parts factory

events.title: Lebanon : Israel strikes key bridge, stoking fears of ground invasion
events.description: Israeli forces destroyed bridges they said were used by Hezbollah. The attack has sparked concern that a large-scale Israeli ground invasion could be in the works, with the Lebanese president calling the bombing 'collective punishment'. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin reports from the ground.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon : Israel strikes key bridge, stoking fears of ground invasion

events.title: Israeli settlers target Palestinian villages in occupied West Bank, attacking people and properties
events.description: The violence began after 18-year-old settler Yehuda Sherman was killed after reportedly being hit by a vehicle driven by a Palestinian while on his quad bike.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israeli settlers target Palestinian villages in occupied West Bank, attacking people and properties

events.title: Dire fertiliser shortage a lurking threat due to Hormuz crisis
events.description: Since the start of the Middle East conflict with Israeli and US strikes on Iran on 28 February, concerns have been growing over rising oil and commodity prices.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Dire fertiliser shortage a lurking threat due to Hormuz crisis

events.title: Middle East war: ‘Force of the law’ must prevail over the law of force, urges Guterres
events.description: The US and Israel must end their bombing campaign before it spirals out of control, while Iran needs to stop attacking Gulf neighbours who have played no direct part in the war roiling the Middle East and beyond, the UN Secretary-General urged on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: ‘Force of the law’ must prevail over the law of force, urges Guterres

events.title: Gabbard testimony on Puerto Rico voting machines raises questions about role of Venezuela conspiracy theory
events.description: National intelligence director said voting machine seizure was requested by US attorney in Puerto Rico – who’s been trying to revive 2020 election conspiracy theory When the US director of national intelligence (DNI), Tulsi Gabbard, testified on Thursday that her office seized voting machines from Puerto Rico , she said it was at the request of the office of the US attorney in Puerto Rico. Left unsaid was that the prosecutor, as the Guardian previously reported , has been the center of a push by Donald Trump supporters to revive a long discredited conspiracy theory purporting to link Venezuela to Trump’s 2020 electoral defeat. Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro, the conspiracy theory maintains, controlled electronic voting machines worldwide and remotely manipulated results in 2020 to deprive Trump of a presidential victory. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Gabbard testimony on Puerto Rico voting machines raises questions about role of Venezuela conspiracy theory

events.title: World News in Brief: Rafah crossing reopens, gender inequality worsens global water crisis, rights defenders in Colombia
events.description: The Rafah crossing into Gaza reopened on Thursday for limited movement in both directions after a 20-day suspension, according to the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA).

events.title: Iran threatens mass ‘water war’ with strikes on key plants in days, UN official warns
events.description: Iran threatens to target desalination and energy infrastructure within days, a U.N. official warns, citing lasting consequences for global markets and water supplies.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran threatens mass ‘water war’ with strikes on key plants in days, UN official warns

events.title: Mexico’s monarch butterfly population jumps 64%, offering hope for at-risk species
events.description: The insects covered its largest area since 2018, despite threats from habitat loss, climate crisis and pesticides The population of monarch butterflies in Mexico increased 64% this winter, compared with the same period in 2025, offering a glimmer of hope for an insect considered at risk of extinction. The figures, released this week by the World Wildlife Fund (WWF) Mexico, showed that the area occupied by monarchs expanded to 2.93 hectares (7.24 acres) of forest from 1.79 hectares (4.42 acres) the previous winter, the largest coverage since 2018. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mexico’s monarch butterfly population jumps 64%, offering hope for at-risk species

events.title: Australia’s generation Alpha faces $185k bill over lifetime without urgent action on climate crisis, report finds
events.description: Global heating consistent with current projections would cost average millennial $130,000 and $165,000 for gen Z, according to Deloitte modelling Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The next generation of Australian workers will cop a $185,000 bill over their lifetimes if the country does not act more urgently to address the climate crisis , according to new modelling by a team of young economists at Deloitte. The new report finds that global heating consistent with the current projections would cost the average millennial approximately $130,000 over the rest of their lives, increasing to $165,000 for gen Z. Continue reading...

events.title: Two killed as Air Canada jet crashes into vehicle, shutting New York's LaGuardia Airport
events.description: A regional passenger jet collided with a ground vehicle upon landing at LaGuardia Airport, prompting a full shutdown and widespread flight disruptions. Both pilots have been killed in the incident with many more injured.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Two killed as Air Canada jet crashes into vehicle, shutting New York's LaGuardia Airport

events.title: Jewish charity's ambulances set on fire. UK counter-terror police probe it as antisemitic attack

events.ticker_label: 💥 Jewish charity's ambulances set on fire. UK counter-terror police probe it as antisemitic attack

events.title: UN calls for solidarity and political will to stamp out racial discrimination
events.description: Nearly 70 years after South African police fired upon peaceful demonstrators in Sharpeville protesting apartheid-era laws, killing 69, the UN renewed the commitment to work for justice and equality on Monday, marking the International Day for the Elimination of Racial Discrimination.

events.title: Amid deepening crisis in Palestine, girls face rising risks and a mounting mental health emergency
events.description: More than two years of unrelenting violence, displacement and loss have pushed children and young people in Palestine into what one UN official describes as a “profound mental health emergency”, with girls facing heightened risks, including a resurgence in child marriage.

events.title: Australia politics live: NSW reveals laws to crush illegal ebikes; Kate Chaney pushes for action 1,000 days after gambling ad ban report
events.description: State legislation due to be introduced on Tuesday will give authorities powers to seize high-powered e-motorbikes. Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor backbencher Ed Husic has called for national laws to manage the risks of artificial intelligence, after thousands of tech workers lost their jobs to the technology. The move placed him at odds with the Albanese government’s approach, which recently scrapped plans for stand-alone AI laws . Governments can’t just be cheerleaders for novel uses of AI, they must prepare and dilute the associated risks. We need a comprehensive, economy-wide national AI act that identifies risks and sets out our expectations for how to manage them and build sorely needed confidence in AI. One thousand days of inaction while online gambling companies flood sports broadcasts with predatory advertising is unacceptable. Every day of delay means more Australians fall victim to an industry that profits from harm and despair. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Australia politics live: NSW reveals laws to crush illegal ebikes; Kate Chaney pushes for action 1,000 days after gambling ad ban report

events.title: 2 buildings collapse in Istanbul after gas explosion, search operations underway
events.description: A natural gas explosion in Istanbul’s Fatih district has brought down two buildings and killed one person

events.title: Israel's war strategy involves high-level assassinations. But will it win the war?

events.title: Reliant on imported fuel, Pacific islands appeal for help as oil prices surge
events.description: Samoa and Tonga raise supply concerns with foreign partners as businesses and residents in Papua New Guinea grapple with higher fuel prices amid the Iran war The leaders of some Pacific countries have appealed for help with oil supplies while others urge against “panic buying” as the import-reliant nations grapple with fears over possible fuel shortages and escalating costs caused by war in the Middle East. Oil prices have surged to nearly $110 a barrel after strikes against energy infrastructure in Iran and the Gulf states. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Reliant on imported fuel, Pacific islands appeal for help as oil prices surge

events.title: Cuba left in darkness after third power cut in a month
events.description: Cuba's power grid has collapsed for the third time this month, leaving the country in darkness with no electricity.

events.title: A city opens its doors to the world: New York and the United Nations
events.description: Born from the ashes of the Second World War, the dream of a more peaceful and fairer world drew nations together in 1945 under a new vision for humanity: the United Nations.

events.title: Air Canada crash at LaGuardia Airport: What happened, who were the victims?

events.title: Ukrainians are growing more pessimistic about the prospects for peace, survey says
events.description: As the Iran war delays peace talks for Ukraine -- and global supplies of air defense missiles -- there are signs that fewer Ukrainians are willing to endure Russia's war for as long as necessary.

events.title: 'Growing mistrust and disagreement' over Trump declaring war with Iran
events.description: In a surprise U-turn, US President Donald Trump has extended a deadline he had given to Iran over the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, adding five days to the initial 48-hour window. He had threatened to strike Iranian power plants were the Strait not to be reopened. For FRANCE 24's Philip Turle, it could be partly explained by the 'growing mistrust and disagreement' over his decision to start a war with Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Growing mistrust and disagreement' over Trump declaring war with Iran

events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN weather agency warns of record ‘climate imbalance’ as planetary warming accelerates

events.title: Colorado River management plans to be determined by government after negotiations stall

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Colorado River management plans to be determined by government after negotiations stall

events.title: Two pilots killed after Air Canada jet collision at LaGuardia in New York
events.description: Nine people hospitalised and airport closed after landing plane hits fire truck responding to separate incident US politics live – latest updates The pilot and co-pilot of an Air Canada Express regional jet have been killed after it collided with a fire truck while landing at New York ’s LaGuardia airport, in an incident that closed the airport for several hours. The collision also caused serious injuries, with nine people in the hospital. It happened as a firefighting vehicle was responding to a separate incident, according to the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey, which runs the airport. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Two pilots killed after Air Canada jet collision at LaGuardia in New York

events.title: The effects of the Iran war on environmental and human health, according to an expert

events.title: How Kyrgyzstan built a system to support domestic violence survivors
events.description: Domestic violence was not something people spoke about openly in Kyrgyzstan in the 1990s following the collapse of the Soviet Union, but after a long road of dedicated efforts, there are now laws addressing family abuse, crisis centres and hotlines while human rights defenders tackle such new challenges as sexual slavery.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Scores feared dead as Colombian military plane crashes
events.description: Transport plane carrying 125 soldiers and crew crashes shortly after takeoff in southern Amazon region Scores of Colombian soldiers are feared dead after a military transport plane crashed on takeoff in the south of the country. The defence minister, Pedro Sánchez, said the accident happened as the Lockheed Martin Hercules C-130 plane was taking off from Puerto Leguízamo , deep in Colombia ’s southern Amazon region, on the border with Peru, as it transported troops from the armed forces. Continue reading...

events.title: Some of the world’s poorest countries to lose UK aid due to 56% budget cut
events.description: UK’s bilateral aid to African countries, which funds areas such as schools and clinics, to be cut by almost £900m by 2028-29 Some of the world’s poorest countries will lose out on UK aid that funds programmes such as schools and clinics, due to budget cuts set out by the foreign secretary. The UK’s bilateral aid to African countries will be reduced by almost £900m by 2028-29 – a 56% cut – as part of more than £6bn in cuts which are funding an increase in defence spending. Continue reading...

events.title: Canadian mother and daughter ‘traumatized’ by ICE detainment, husband says
events.description: Tania Warner and Ayla, her seven-year-old with autism, sent to notorious Texas detention center and told to ‘self-deport’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A Canadian woman and her seven-year-old daughter with autism who have been held by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement ( ICE ) for nearly a week have been transferred to a notorious detention center and asked to “self-deport”, according to her husband, who said the pair had been “traumatized” by the experience. Tania Warner and her daughter Ayla Luca, originally from British Columbia, moved to the US five years ago, when Warner married Edward Warner, a US citizen. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Canadian mother and daughter ‘traumatized’ by ICE detainment, husband says

events.title: Trump says the U.S. is in talks with Iran to end the war, which Iran denies

events.title: UK police probe anti-Semitic attack on Jewish ambulances

Police said the attack is being treated as an anti-Semitic hate crime.

events.title: Middle East war shockwaves ripple through Asia-Pacific fuel and supply chains
events.description: The fallout from the war in the Middle East is rippling far beyond the Gulf, disrupting fuel supplies, shipping routes and supply chains across Asia and the Pacific, with some of the region’s most vulnerable economies already feeling the strain through rising prices, rationing and threats to jobs, food security and remittances.

events.title: Police investigating Alabama student Jimmy Gracey’s death pursue no charges as they await toxicology, autopsy

events.title: New Zealand PM’s ratings dip as fragile economy fails to impress before November election, poll shows

The RNZ-Reid Research poll , released on Monday, also found a growing number of people felt that New Zealand was heading in the wrong direction. Continue reading...

events.title: BTS release new album Arirang ahead of comeback concert
events.description: Boyband drops album that speaks to its Korean roots ahead of Seoul comeback concert, with more than a quarter of a million fans expected to attend K-pop stars BTS released a new album on Friday billed as reflecting the maturing boy band’s Korean roots and identity, as buzz built ahead of their open-air comeback concert in the heart of Seoul. The Saturday night gig, which is expected to draw around 260,000 people, will be BTS’s first after a hiatus of almost four years while all seven members served compulsory military service. It comes ahead of an 82-date world tour. Continue reading...

events.title: ABC journalists to strike for first time in 20 years with widespread news disruption expected
events.description: Union says below‑inflation pay rises and insecure work threaten the future of Australia’s public‑interest journalism Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Sign up for Guardian Australia’s free weekly media newsletter here ABC journalists will walk off the job on Wednesday for the first time in 20 years, triggering severe disruption to the public broadcaster’s news services for 24 hours. The protected industrial action involves staff in the journalists’ Media, Entertainment and Arts Alliance (MEAA) and the non-journalists’ Community and Public Sector Union (CPSU), which represents staff in technology and control systems. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ABC journalists to strike for first time in 20 years with widespread news disruption expected

events.title: Woman has sentence quashed by Tanzania court after over a decade on death row
events.description: Lemi Limbu, who has severe intellectual disabilities, remains in prison and will now face retrial for the murder of her daughter A woman with severe intellectual disabilities in Tanzania has had her conviction and death sentence quashed after spending more than a decade in prison awaiting execution. Lemi Limbu , now in her early 30s, was convicted of the murder of her daughter in 2015. On 4 March, a court in Shinyanga, northern Tanzania, declared she can appeal. She will face a retrial, but a date has yet to be set. Continue reading...

Andrew Boe represented Doomadgee’s family and the Palm Island community council at an aborted coronial inquest in 2005 and then in subsequent inquests in 2006 and 2010. Continue reading...

events.title: Whose war is it? Trump eyes Iran talks as Israel hints at long campaign
events.description: Another deadline day, another ultimatum, another surprise turn from the US president. The same Donald Trump who may or may not send ground troops to try and secure the Strait of Hormuz now affirming that his administration's talking with the Iranians and thus holding off on hitting critical infrastructure. Trump's messaging since the start 24 days ago keeping friends and foes alike guessing. That's in stark contrast to Israeli allies, who in words and in deeds seem to be embracing a fight many of its leaders have been dreaming for decades.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Whose war is it? Trump eyes Iran talks as Israel hints at long campaign

events.title: After strong showing in France's major cities, will left even qualify for 2027 presidential runoff?
events.description: Carys Garland is pleased to welcome Renaud Foucart, Economist, Senior Lecturer at Lancaster University. He observes a political landscape shaped less by ideology alone and more by geography and fragmentation. What these municipal elections reveal is not a clear national trajectory, but a mosaic of local preferences. The National Rally, while dominant in national polling, remains territorially constrained, while the left finds itself caught in a structural paradox. To reach the second round of a presidential election, it must unify, without alienating the moderate electorate.

events.title: Israel destroys another bridge linking south Lebanon to rest of country
events.description: As it engages in heated battles with pro-Iran militant group Hezbollah in a number of Lebanese border villages, Israel has bombed another bridge across a river dividing the country's north and south. It adds to widespread fears in Lebanon that Israel intends to depopulate the southern region in the long-term as a million people have been displaced by the fighting. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin tells us more from Beirut.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Israel destroys another bridge linking south Lebanon to rest of country

events.title: Italian voters reject Giorgia Meloni's judicial reform in referendum defeat
events.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni conceded defeat on Monday in a referendum which sought to overhaul the country's judiciary. The vote is a major blow to Meloni's far-right leadership, and could weaken her re-election chances next year.

events.title: Italy divided over Meloni government, referendum exit polls show
events.description: Polls have closed in Italy's two-day referendum. According to exit polls, the "No" vote is slightly ahead - but the final results could differ, as they do not take into account what Italians from abroad voted. What can be said though, is that the country 'is very divided' over the county's leadership, says FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta from Rome.

events.title: Madagascar’s military ruler decrees that ministers must pass lie detector tests
events.description: Michael Randrianirina, who sacked PM and cabinet without explanation, claims measure is to root out corruption Madagascar’s military president has said new ministers will have to pass lie detector tests to root out corrupt candidates, after he dismissed the prime minister and cabinet without explanation earlier this month. Michael Randrianirina came to power in a coup in October after weeks of youth-led protests under the banner “Gen Z Madagascar”. However, young people were quickly disenchanted by his choice of government officials, which they saw as being part of the old, corrupt elite. Continue reading...

events.title: Dr. Andrew Smith: Elections are won by 'constructing a vision that articulates political values'
events.description: Stuart Norval is pleased to welcome Dr. Andrew Smith, Historian of Modern France and Lecturer at Queen Mary University of London. What stands out to our guest is not just that the Socialists held Paris, but how they did so: by resisting opportunistic alliances and instead presenting a coherent, independent platform rooted in credibility and local recognition. According to Dr. Smith, the narrative of far-right inevitability is overstated. While they continue to make strategic inroads in small cities, these results demonstrate that their ascent is neither uniform nor guaranteed.

events.title: Red Command: Brazil's most powerful criminal organisation
events.description: In October 2025, a police operation left 122 people dead in the favelas of Penha and Alemão, in the north of Rio de Janeiro. Its aim was to weaken the Comando Vermelho, or "Red Command", one of Brazil's oldest and most powerful criminal organisations. The gang is growing rapidly; in 16 years it has increased its area of control by 90 percent and is extending its influence far beyond Brazil.

To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Some ships find ways to sail through the Strait of Hormuz as hundreds are stalled
events.description: THe Strait of Hormuz isn't entirely closed -- some ships are getting through as Iran flexes its control over the strategic waterway.

events.title: Italy's far-right Prime Minister's political future at stake in referendum vote
events.description: Italian Prime Minister's close relationship with President Trump may weigh on a referendum on reforming Italy's judiciary.

events.title: Asian markets tumble, but Europe, US rally on Trump optimism
events.description: Markets in Asia nosedived as trading restarted on Monday. Europe's indices were headed in the same direction, until optimistic Iran comments from Donald Trump prompted traders in Europe and the US to start buying again.

events.title: France: Former PM Lionel Jospin dies aged 88
events.description: Jospin proved the canary in the coal mine for the far-right rise in France. He was the first center-left presidential candidate to finish behind Jean-Marie Le Pen but rallied his supporters to his rival, Jacques Chirac.
events.ticker_label: 💥 France: Former PM Lionel Jospin dies aged 88

events.title: German court rejects climate case against BMW, Mercedes
events.description: Environmental campaigners wanted to stop the companies from selling combustion engine cars after 2030. They argued that continuing to release high amounts of CO2 could infringe on the rights of younger generations.

events.title: China-North Korea transit relaunch points to better ties
events.description: Passenger air and rail links are being restored between China and its North Korean ally as geopolitical upheavals add new impetus to improving the relationship between Pyongyang and Beijing.

events.title: Iranians and Israelis united through music
events.description: The Berlin-based music ensemble Sistanagila brings together musicians from Iran and Israel who offer a message of hope for a better future.

events.title: Alexandra Popp and Borussia Dortmund: A sign of the times
events.description: Germany's most famous female footballer has signed for a third-division team. The transfer will have huge impacts across the women's game in Germany.

events.title: Germany news: Pistorius brushes off SPD leadership talk
events.description: Defense Minister Boris Pistorius has ruled out a change of leadership at the top of his center-left Social Democrats after an electoral slump. Meanwhile, two major automakers have won a legal battle.

events.title: Ilya Remeslo, formerly a pro-Putin blogger, has ended up in a Russian psychiatric hospital after criticizing the president. What happened?
events.description: After seemingly switching sides, a pro-government blogger in Russia who criticized President Vladimir Putin has been admitted to psychiatric care in St. Petersburg. Did he go rogue?

events.title: Lionel Jospin (1937-2026): 'Highly-respected moral and intellectual figure among left wing'
events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Damien Lecomte, Political Scientist and Researcher at CRPS-CESSP, Paris Pantheon Sorbonne University. According to Dr. Lecomte, there is a persistent tension between unity and fragmentation in French politics, and the legacy of Lionel Jospin illustrates this paradox: a figure of moral integrity and intellectual rigor who successfully governed a broad left-wing coalition, yet he was ultimately defeated by a divided electorate on the left.

events.title: ‘Raulito’ Castro, the Trump administration’s wild card in Cuba
events.description: As Trump ratchets up the pressure on Cuba, Raul Rodriguez Castro – the grandson of Raul Castro – is said to be an emerging figure in dealings with the US administration on opening up the Cuban economy.

events.title: Who is Emmanuel Grégoire, Paris's next mayor?
events.description: With more than 50% of the vote, Socialist Emmanuel Grégoire was elected as the next mayor of Paris.FRANCE 24 takes a closer look at the political career of the 48-year-old, who was seen as incumbent Mayor Anne Hidalgo's right hand man until a public falling out in 2022.

events.title: EU restricts Hungary's access to classified intelligence over alleged leaks to Russia
events.description: As European far-right leaders gather around Hungarian PM Viktor Orban, the EU has restricted classified intelligence to Hungary following allegations that the Hungarian foreign minister leaked EU Council details to his Russian counterpart. Since this has come out, several European leaders have said they had suspected this had been the case for some time. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley looks into these revelations, and what that could mean for the upcoming Hungarian elections.
events.ticker_label: 💥 EU restricts Hungary's access to classified intelligence over alleged leaks to Russia

events.title: Former French PM, father of 35-hour work week, dies aged 88
events.description: Former French prime minister Lionel Jospin has died ages 88. The socialist was the father of the 35-hour work week and civil partnerships for gay couples, among other major social reforms. But he is also remembered for his loss in the first round of the 2002 presidential election against far-right leader Jean-Marie Le Pen.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Former French PM, father of 35-hour work week, dies aged 88

events.title: Owner of OnlyFans adult platform dies at the age of 43
events.description: Leonid Radvinsky, a Ukrainian-American entrepreneur who was the owner of the adult content platform OnlyFans, has died.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Owner of OnlyFans adult platform dies at the age of 43

events.title: British couple detained in Iran feel 'let down' and call on Starmer to 'help us'
events.description: A British couple jailed for 10 years in Iran on "falsified" spying charges say they feel abandoned by the UK government - and have urged the prime minister to "step up and help us".
events.ticker_label: 💥 British couple detained in Iran feel 'let down' and call on Starmer to 'help us'

events.title: Lindsay Hoyle demands fraudster who fled to Tenerife faces justice
events.description: It is "outrageous" that a convicted fraudster tracked down by Sky News in Tenerife is still on the run, the Speaker of the House of Commons has said.

events.title: Emmanuel Grégoire becomes Paris' new mayor
events.description: Emmanuel Grégoire has won the Paris mayoral race and takes over from fellow Socialist Anne Hidalgo

events.title: Police find cocaine worth $12M on drug-smuggling boat off Puerto Rico
events.description: Cocaine with an estimated value of $12 million has been seized off the north coast of Puerto Rico, authorities said

events.title: Italy’s justice referendum becomes a high stakes test for conservative Premier Giorgia Meloni
events.description: Italy’s conservative premier, Giorgia Meloni, faces a pivotal political test in a two-day referendum on judicial reform

events.title: Notorious apartheid police commander testifies at South African hearing on killings
events.description: One of South Africa’s most notorious apartheid police commanders has testified at a new inquiry into the 1985 killing of four anti-apartheid activists in South Africa
events.ticker_label: 💥 Notorious apartheid police commander testifies at South African hearing on killings

events.title: 50 years after Argentina’s bloody coup, families still search for the disappeared
events.description: Nearly 50 years after Argentina's 1976 coup that led to a repressive dictatorship, families are still searching for and burying the disappeared

events.title: Europe's far right backs Orbán as Hungary's coming election puts his brand to the test
events.description: European far-right leaders are rallying around Hungary’s Viktor Orbán as his grip on power looks shakier than it has in years

events.title: North Korea says summit with Japan is off unless Tokyo drops 'its anachronistic' ways
events.description: The powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un says that a summit between her brother and Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi won’t happen if Japan sticks to “its anachronistic” approach

events.title: Italian voters reject judicial reform in a setback for Premier Giorgia Meloni
events.description: Italian voters have rejected a Meloni-backed judicial reform in a referendum, handing the right-wing government a major political setback

events.title: Spring break hot spots turn lawless as fights erupt, drugs flow and dozens arrested in sweeping crackdowns
events.description: Fort Lauderdale and Daytona Beach racked up dozens of spring break arrests this weekend as Florida cities impose curfews and crack down on beach chaos.

events.title: Shanann Watts' father says family faced years of 'evil' online hate after Chris Watts murders
events.description: Shanann Watts' family says they've faced relentless harassment and conspiracy theories since Chris Watts' 2018 murder conviction, calling it "evil."

events.title: 9 suspects arrested after $7M in stolen cargo seized in Southern California bust
events.description: Nine suspects were arrested after authorities recovered $7 million in stolen cargo and $1 million in cash as part of a cargo theft bust in California.

events.title: Chicago residents unionize to fight possible displacement, rent hikes over Obama Presidential Center
events.description: Woodlawn tenants at Chaney Braggs Apartments have formed a union to fight displacement they say is driven by the Obama Presidential Center's arrival.

events.title: EU blocks US vote to define gender as biological men and women at UN women’s forum
events.description: The EU used a procedural motion to block a U.S. resolution at the United Nations that sought to define gender as referring to biological men and women, leaving it without a vote.

events.title: Carlos Fernández de Cossío: Cuba 'preparing' for 'possibility of military aggression'
events.description: Cuba's deputy foreign minister says the military is preparing for possible U.S. aggression after Trump said it would be an "honor" to take Cuba.

events.title: Dad loses custody of autistic son after fighting sex change, gets support from Elon Musk
events.description: Father in Iceland says he lost custody after opposing his 11-year-old autistic son's sex reassignment, claiming courts prioritized ideology over parental rights.

events.title: Families of Iran's elite live lavishly abroad while ordinary citizens suffer at home
events.description: Critics say children of Iran's ruling elite live freely in the West while their families enforce strict rules at home, with calls to sanction them.

events.title: Live possum discovered hiding among plush toys in an Australian airport gift shop
events.description: A wild possum found a clever hiding spot among plush toys in an airport gift shop in Australia and was safely removed, staff confirmed.
